Defines the `OptionMenu` structure and related functions for creating and managing option menus.

# Purpose
The code defines a C header file for managing an `OptionMenu` interface, which is part of a user interface library. The `OptionMenu` structure is designed to represent a menu with selectable options, and it includes various components such as a `Window`, `StatusBarLayer`, and `MenuLayer`. The menu can display a title, handle different content types, and manage visual elements like icons and colors. The file provides a set of function prototypes and type definitions to configure and manipulate the `OptionMenu`, including setting callbacks for menu interactions, configuring colors, and managing the menu's lifecycle.

The `OptionMenu` is highly configurable through the `OptionMenuConfig` structure and various setter functions. Callbacks are used to define custom behavior for menu selection, drawing, and unloading. The code also includes functions to initialize and deinitialize the menu, as well as to create and destroy `OptionMenu` instances. The `OptionMenuContentType` enumeration specifies the layout of the menu content, allowing for single-line or multi-line options. This header file is intended to be included in other C source files to provide a consistent interface for creating and managing option menus within an application.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `applib/ui/ui.h`


# Data Structures

---
### OptionMenu
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `window`: Holds the window where the option menu is displayed.
    - `status_layer`: Represents the status bar layer of the option menu.
    - `menu_layer`: Contains the menu layer for displaying menu items.
    - `title`: Points to the title of the option menu.
    - `title_font`: Specifies the font used for the title.
    - `content_type`: Defines the type of content layout in the menu.
    - `chosen_image`: Stores the image used for a chosen option.
    - `not_chosen_image`: Stores the image used for an unchosen option.
    - `icons_enabled`: Indicates if icons are enabled in the menu.
    - `callbacks`: Holds the set of callback functions for menu actions.
    - `context`: Points to user-defined data passed to callbacks.
    - `choice`: Stores the current choice index in the menu.
    - `status_colors`: Defines the colors for the status bar.
    - `normal_colors`: Defines the colors for normal menu items.
    - `highlight_colors`: Defines the colors for highlighted menu items.
- **Description**: Represents an option menu structure that includes a window, status bar, and menu layer for displaying options. It supports customization through title, font, and content type, and allows for the use of images to indicate chosen or unchosen options. The structure also includes callback functions for handling menu actions and user-defined context data. Color settings for different menu states and an indicator for enabling icons are also part of the structure.


---
### OptionMenuCallbacks
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `select`: A callback function for handling menu item selection.
    - `get_num_rows`: A callback function to get the number of rows in the menu.
    - `draw_row`: A callback function to draw a row in the menu.
    - `unload`: A callback function for unloading resources when the menu is closed.
    - `get_cell_height`: A callback function to get the height of a cell in the menu.
- **Description**: Defines a set of callback functions for managing the behavior and appearance of an option menu, including selection handling, row drawing, and resource unloading.


---
### OptionMenuColors
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - `background`: Specifies the background color using the `GColor` type.
    - `foreground`: Specifies the foreground color using the `GColor` type.
- **Description**: Defines a color scheme for an option menu, consisting of a background and a foreground color, both represented by the `GColor` type.


---
### OptionMenuContentType
- **Type**: ``enum``
- **Members**:
    - ``OptionMenuContentType_Default``: Content consists of a title and subtitle or a single-line title with ample vertical spacing.
    - ``OptionMenuContentType_SingleLine``: Content consists of a single line.
    - ``OptionMenuContentType_DoubleLine``: Content consists of two lines.
    - ``OptionMenuContentTypeCount``: Represents the count of content types available.
- **Description**: Defines different types of content layouts for an option menu, specifying whether the content is a single line, double line, or includes a title and subtitle with specific spacing.


---
### OptionMenuConfig
- **Type**: ``struct``
- **Members**:
    - ``title``: A pointer to a constant character string that represents the title of the option menu.
    - ``choice``: An integer that stores the current choice or selection index in the option menu.
    - ``content_type``: An enumeration that specifies the type of content layout for the option menu.
    - ``status_colors``: A structure that defines the background and foreground colors for the status area of the option menu.
    - ``highlight_colors``: A structure that defines the background and foreground colors for the highlighted item in the option menu.
    - ``icons_enabled``: A boolean that indicates whether icons are enabled in the option menu.
- **Description**: Defines the configuration settings for an option menu, including its title, current choice, content layout type, color settings for status and highlight areas, and whether icons are enabled.

### option\_menu\_default\_cell\_height<!-- {{#callable_declaration:option_menu_default_cell_height}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/option_menu_window.h#L91>)

Calculates the default cell height for an option menu.
- **Description**: Use this function to get the default height of a cell in an option menu based on the content type and selection state. This function is useful when you need to determine the appropriate cell height for rendering menu items. It considers whether the menu item is selected and adjusts the height accordingly. Ensure that the `content_type` is a valid `OptionMenuContentType` value.
- **Inputs**:
    - `content_type`: Specifies the type of content in the menu cell. Must be a valid `OptionMenuContentType` enumeration value.
    - `selected`: Indicates whether the menu item is currently selected. Accepts `true` or `false`.
- **Output**: Returns the calculated cell height as a `uint16_t` value.
- **See Also**: [`option_menu_default_cell_height`](<option_menu_window.c.md#option_menu_default_cell_height>)  (Implementation)

### option\_menu\_set\_status\_colors<!-- {{#callable_declaration:option_menu_set_status_colors}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/option_menu_window.h#L93>)

Sets the status bar colors for an option menu.
- **Description**: Use this function to change the background and foreground colors of the status bar in an `OptionMenu`. This function must be called with a valid `OptionMenu` pointer. It updates the status bar's appearance immediately, reflecting the specified colors. Ensure that the `OptionMenu` is properly initialized before calling this function to avoid undefined behavior.
- **Inputs**:
    - `option_menu`: A pointer to an `OptionMenu` structure. Must not be null. The function modifies the status colors of this menu.
    - `background`: A `GColor` value representing the new background color for the status bar. Must be a valid color.
    - `foreground`: A `GColor` value representing the new foreground color for the status bar. Must be a valid color.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`option_menu_set_status_colors`](<option_menu_window.c.md#option_menu_set_status_colors>)  (Implementation)

- **Inputs**:
    - `option_menu`: A pointer to an `OptionMenu` structure. Must not be null. The function modifies the color settings of this menu.
    - `background`: A `GColor` value representing the background color to set for the normal state. Must be a valid color.
    - `foreground`: A `GColor` value representing the foreground color to set for the normal state. Must be a valid color.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`option_menu_set_normal_colors`](<option_menu_window.c.md#option_menu_set_normal_colors>)  (Implementation)

Assigns callback functions and context to an option menu.
- **Description**: Use this function to set the callback functions and context for an `OptionMenu` instance. This function must be called to define how the option menu behaves when interacting with its items. Ensure that the `option_menu` parameter is a valid pointer to an `OptionMenu` instance and that `callbacks` is a valid pointer to an `OptionMenuCallbacks` structure containing the desired callback functions. The `context` parameter can be used to pass additional data to the callbacks. This function does not perform any validation on the input parameters, so ensure they are correctly initialized before calling.
- **Inputs**:
    - `option_menu`: A pointer to an `OptionMenu` instance. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `callbacks`: A pointer to an `OptionMenuCallbacks` structure containing the callback functions. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data that will be passed to the callback functions. Can be null. The caller retains ownership.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`option_menu_set_callbacks`](<option_menu_window.c.md#option_menu_set_callbacks>)  (Implementation)

The `title` parameter must be a valid, null-terminated string, and the caller retains ownership of this string.

    - `option_menu`: A pointer to an `OptionMenu` instance. Must not be null and must point to a valid, initialized `OptionMenu`.

Sets the current choice in the option menu.
- **Description**: Use this function to update the selected choice in an `OptionMenu` instance. This function marks the menu layer as dirty, which triggers a redraw of the menu. Ensure that the `OptionMenu` instance is properly initialized before calling this function. This function does not validate the `choice` value, so ensure that the value is within the valid range for the menu.
- **Inputs**:
    - `option_menu`: A pointer to an `OptionMenu` instance. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership.
    - `choice`: An integer representing the new choice to set. There are no internal checks for validity, so ensure the value is within the valid range for the menu.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`option_menu_set_choice`](<option_menu_window.c.md#option_menu_set_choice>)  (Implementation)

Ensure that the `OptionMenu` pointer is valid before calling this function.

Enable or disable radio button icons in an option menu.

This function does not perform any validation on the `OptionMenu` pointer, so ensure that it is valid before calling. The function does not return a value and has no side effects other than modifying the `icons_enabled` state of the `OptionMenu`.
- **Inputs**:
    - `option_menu`: A pointer to an `OptionMenu` structure. Must not be null. The caller retains ownership and is responsible for ensuring it points to a valid `OptionMenu`.

This function must be called after the `OptionMenu` has been initialized and used, and before the `OptionMenu` instance is destroyed or goes out of scope.

### option\_menu\_destroy<!-- {{#callable_declaration:option_menu_destroy}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/option_menu_window.h#L123>)

Releases resources associated with an `OptionMenu`.
- **Description**: Use this function to free all resources associated with an `OptionMenu` object when it is no longer needed. This function must be called to prevent memory leaks after you finish using an `OptionMenu`. Ensure that the `option_menu` parameter is a valid pointer to an `OptionMenu` object that has been initialized. Do not use the `OptionMenu` after calling this function, as it will be in an undefined state.
- **Inputs**:
    - `option_menu`: A pointer to an `OptionMenu` object. Must not be null and must point to a valid, initialized `OptionMenu`. The function will not check for null pointers, and passing a null pointer will result in undefined behavior.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`option_menu_destroy`](<option_menu_window.c.md#option_menu_destroy>)  (Implementation)

### option\_menu\_system\_draw\_row<!-- {{#callable_declaration:option_menu_system_draw_row}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../../src/fw/applib/ui/option_menu_window.h#L124>)

Draws a row in the option menu with specified text and style.
- **Description**: Use this function to render a row in an option menu with a given title and style. It must be called within a valid drawing context, typically during a menu layer's draw callback. The function adjusts text alignment and overflow based on the device's shape and the presence of icons. Ensure that the `option_menu` is properly initialized before calling this function.
- **Inputs**:
    - `option_menu`: A pointer to an `OptionMenu` structure. Must not be null and must be initialized before use.
    - `ctx`: A pointer to a `GContext` used for drawing. Must not be null.
    - `cell_layer`: A pointer to a `Layer` representing the cell to draw in. Must not be null.
    - `cell_frame`: A pointer to a `GRect` defining the frame of the cell. Must not be null.
    - `title`: A string representing the title to draw. Can be null, in which case no text is drawn.
    - `selected`: A boolean indicating if the row is selected, affecting its appearance.
    - `context`: A pointer to user-defined data. Can be null.
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`option_menu_system_draw_row`](<option_menu_window.c.md#option_menu_system_draw_row>)  (Implementation)
